metadata.title:
AR[ VIEWING


LOCAL SITES

Engineer ellis Men Run
Lines for the New


Reservoir.

pages.raw_text:
City Engineer Gill sent a force of as
sistants out this morning to run lines
and return figures regarding different
prospective sites for the new 10000, 000,-
gallon city water reservoir.


The first site inspected is that near
the up-river pumping station. The men
are taking the ground elevations and
are making Typographical outline of
the site and the lay of the pipe lines
that would connect the reservoir with
the city system.


1t is estimated that the new reservoir
will be 5l6 feet in length and 25S feet
in width, Around the reservoir is to
be roadway either so or IS feet in
width. Accordingly the reservoir will
approximately cover two city blocks.


Mr.. ellis favorite sites would be
near the city. He has in view loca.
ton one mile southeast of Riverside
avenue and Howard street. A rough
estimate would make this site cost
5000, "a city lots of anestimated value
of $500 per lot. One of the leading
reasons for Mr.. ellis favoring this
site is that it would furnish an extra
supply of water for the city in case
the chief water main leading into town
from the up-river station should break


Other sites held in view by the en-
gineering department would cost from
$500 to $4,000.


A new plan mentioned this morning
was to get site on the hilltop to the
south of town. Such site would be
at too great an elevation for the estim
ated capacity of the pipes under exist
ing conditions, but it is believed this
fault could be overcome by putting in
n power wheel.


The result of Engineer ellis est
mates will be placed before the boart
of public works, and in turn will proha
bly, go before the city council at the
next meeting.
